greeting: Halt, pilgrim. None pass beyond this threshold without answering for the road behind them.
I have stood at this gate since before your grandfather drew breath. State your purpose, or turn back.
The Grail does not call the timid. Speak, and let me judge whether the Road has forged you or broken you.
quest: Many have walked the King's Road believing the Grail awaits the strongest arm or the cleverest tongue. They lie in unmarked graves along the way. Tell me — what mercy did you show at Ravensgate? What did you sacrifice at the Pilgrim's Rest? The Grail remembers what you have done, even if you do not.

I was the last knight of the Grail company. My brothers fell to pride, to despair, to the slow forgetting of why we set out. I alone remain, not because I was the greatest, but because I never stopped asking whether I was worthy. That question is the only key to this door.
idle: The wind through these ruins carries voices. Some days I hear my brothers singing the old hymns.
I have refused kings and saints alike. The Grail's standard is not rank, but readiness.
You linger. Good. The impatient never pass.
